table.note: The surface the sheets lie on. Visible only in the seam between them, never behind content. The one warm colour in the product, and the only one exempt from the grayscale ink rule.

tinta-0.note: Named after the ballpoint it came from. Resolves to an ink on either sheet under the grayscale rule; applies in full outside them.

verde-0.note: Semantic only: purchase confirmed, price recorded.

headline.purpose: Screen titles in list header and dashboard.

body.purpose: Default UI text. 15px is the floor for content text; labels, meta and secondary figures go smaller by design and are governed by contrast instead.

hand.purpose: Unpurchased item names — what the household wrote. TARGET; face provisional, a runtime appearance setting is planned.

data.purpose: Purchased item names, all prices, totals, EANs. Always tabular-nums.
data.measuredCapHeight: 12.95px at 17.5px size — matched to Hand by cap height, not px

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Card lifted off the stack; suggestion popover.

shadows.name: cast-list
shadows.purpose: TARGET. The ~80 g/m2 list sheet casting onto the table.

shadows.name: cast-receipt
shadows.purpose: TARGET. The ~50 g/m2 receipt — thinner stock, shallower cast.

shadows.name: rim-receipt
shadows.purpose: TARGET. Rim light on the receipt's cut edge. Must be a layer above the paper canvas AND the multiply veil — an inset box-shadow paints beneath an element's children and is covered.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus. Applied to the wrapper, not the raw input.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Sheet open; buy-again chip. The only overshoot in the system.

hitTargets.name: hit-min
hitTargets.purpose: WCAG/iOS minimum. The floor, not the target.

paper.note: TARGET. Procedural low-poly crease. Delaunay over a jittered grid, flat-shaded per facet; per-vertex height from 2-octave value noise, lit by one distant lamp. Deterministic from seed.

amplitude.why: A multiplier yields no usable range on a near-black paper.

veil.why: Multiply scales the crease instead of replacing it, so contrast survives — and multiply is what a cast shadow physically is.

paper.scope: One canvas per sheet, never per row. The canvas covers the sheet including its header.

receipt.why: Receipt grows with every purchase; keyed to height it is ~14400 verts at 200 items. Tile is generated once from the seed and repeated.

list.why: Never had the cost problem, carries no veil, and a long fold reads across it. Tiling would show here first.

tiling.constraint: Tile must wrap toroidally: ghost points across each edge of the jittered grid, value noise periodic at the tile size. 512/16 = 32 cells exactly; keep that divisible if either number moves.
tiling.rejected: Viewport-windowed generation - constant cost, but moves it from the purchase tap onto scroll.
tiling.risk: Periodicity, ~19 repeats down a 200-item receipt. Amplitude is +/-10 absolute RGB in light (7.8% peak-to-peak, near the visibility floor) but +/-20 in dark (15.7%). Judge tiling in dark mode.

paper.rejected: feDisplacementMap text warping via a facet normal map. Implemented and working (blur the map first or glyphs tear at facet edges), ~24ms to generate, but the filter re-evaluates on every paint and the list scrolls, polls and animates.

layout.note: There are no width breakpoints. The app is fluid and mobile-first; the only width constraint is a content cap. Do not author min-width/max-width media queries -- the CSS carries none, and the only media queries in the product are prefers-color-scheme and prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one filled control. Accent ground, no lift, no glow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); color: var(--accent-fg, #eef1f5); font-family: var(--font-sans, 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; min-height: 44px; padding: 9px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--tinta-1, #2c56c1); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgb(26 63 160 / 30%); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: var(--paper-edge, #b4bac4); cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Horizontally scrolling filter. Pill, hairline, selected state fills with accent.
components.css: .ds-chips { display: flex; gap: 6px; } .ds-chip { flex-shrink: 0; padding: 4px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--paper-edge, #b4bac4); border-radius: 999px; background: var(--paper-1, #e3e7ed); font-family: var(--font-sans, 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 13px; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); cursor: pointer; } .ds-chip.is-on { background: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); border-color: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); color: var(--accent-fg, #eef1f5); }

components.name: Smart Input Bar
components.description: Sticky bottom entry with the sigil legend above it. The focus ring lands on the wrapper so the whole control reads as focused.
components.html:
+ cant.# marca@ tienda| cod. barras
Añadir producto…
components.css: .ds-sib { font-family: var(--font-sans, 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif); } .ds-sib-legend { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-sib-chip { font-size: 12px; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); background: var(--paper-1, #e3e7ed); border: 1px solid var(--paper-edge, #b4bac4); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 7px; } .ds-sib-chip b { color: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); } .ds-sib-row { display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: center; background: var(--paper-1, #e3e7ed); border: 1.5px solid var(--paper-edge, #b4bac4); border-radius: 14px; padding: 10px 12px; } .ds-sib-row:focus-within { border-color: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); } .ds-sib-field { flex: 1; font-size: 16px; color: var(--ink-2, #6a6d7a); } .ds-sib-add { width: var(--hit-min, 44px); height: var(--hit-min, 44px); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); color: var(--accent-fg, #eef1f5); border: none; font-size: 22px; line-height: 1; cursor: pointer; }

components.name: Item Row — Unpurchased
components.description: TARGET. An instruction: how many, which brand, which shop. Hand face, uppercase, no price.
components.html:
LECHE ENTERA
×6·HACENDADO·CARREFOUR
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; gap: 11px; align-items: center; padding: 9px 14px; } .ds-box { width: 21px; height: 21px; border-radius: 50%; border: 2px solid var(--paper-edge, #b4bac4); flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-body { flex: 1; min-width: 0; } .ds-name { font-family: var(--font-written, 'Patrick Hand SC', cursive); font-size: 18.9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.045em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-0, #15161b); line-height: 1.25; } .ds-meta { display: flex; gap: 5px; margin-top: 2px; font-family: var(--font-written, 'Patrick Hand SC', cursive); font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-2, #6a6d7a); } .ds-qty { color: var(--ink-1, #353742); }

components.name: Item Row — Purchased
components.description: TARGET. A record: what was actually bought and what it cost. Monospace, uppercase, amount right-aligned. No strikethrough.
components.html:
CAFÉ MOLIDO
500 G · LIDL
5,0010,00 / kg
components.css: .ds-prow { display: flex; gap: 11px; align-items: center; padding: 9px 14px; } .ds-pbox { width: 21px; height: 21px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--verde-0, #2f7a4a); flex-shrink: 0; position: relative; } .ds-pbox::after { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; width: 9px; height: 5px; border-left: 2px solid var(--paper-0, #eef1f5); border-bottom: 2px solid var(--paper-0, #eef1f5); transform: translate(-50%, -62%) rotate(-45deg); } .ds-pbody { flex: 1; min-width: 0; } .ds-pname { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 17.5px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); } .ds-pmeta { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 12.5px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-2, #6a6d7a); margin-top: 2px; } .ds-pamt { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-pmain { display: block; font-size: 16.5px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); } .ds-psub { display: block; font-size: 11px; color: var(--ink-2, #6a6d7a); margin-top: 1px; }

components.name: Pre-printed Sheet Title
components.description: TARGET. Both sheets share one pre-printed voice — the app's serif with a dashed rule, as on stock printed before anything is written on it.
components.css: .ds-title { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; margin: 0 14px 6px; padding: 13px 0 7px; font-family: var(--font-display, 'Bree Serif', Georgia, serif); font-size: 15px; font-weight: 400; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); border-bottom: 1px dashed rgb(148 150 159 / 62%); } .ds-title-cost { font-family: var(--font-display, 'Bree Serif', Georgia, serif); font-size: 15px; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Fixed above the input bar, respecting the safe area. A draining progress bar reads as time remaining.
components.html: Leche entera añadida
components.css: .ds-toast { border-radius: 14px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 4px rgb(30 35 55 / 14%), 0 16px 32px -8px rgb(30 35 55 / 24%); font-family: var(--font-sans, 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif); } .ds-toast-bar { height: 3px; background: rgb(47 122 74 / 32%); } .ds-toast-fill { height: 100%; width: 60%; background: var(--verde-0, #2f7a4a); border-radius: 0 2px 2px 0; } .ds-toast-body { background: var(--paper-0, #eef1f5); border: 1px solid rgb(47 122 74 / 32%); border-top: none; padding: 11px 14px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-toast-text { flex: 1; font-size: 14px; color: var(--ink-1, #353742); } .ds-toast-text strong { color: var(--ink-0, #15161b); font-weight: 600; } .ds-toast-cta { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--tinta-0, #1a3fa0); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; }

narrative.overview:
El ticket is what a receipt is called in Spain, and it is the whole idea. This is not a shopping-list app that happens to store prices; it is a list that becomes a receipt as you shop. Before the shop, a line is an instruction — six of these, that brand, that shop. After the shop, the same line is a record — what was actually bought, what it actually cost. Two different documents, so two different sheets of paper, in two different hands.

That resolves what the interface is made of. You write the list; the till prints the receipt. So the list is set in a handwriting face in block capitals, and the receipt in monospace, also in capitals — the same case arrived at from two unrelated causes, which is exactly why the two can share it without the distinction collapsing. Both sheets carry their titles in the app's serif, because on real stock the header is pre-printed before anything is written on it.

The palette is cool blue-grey paper and near-black ink, on a warm wooden table. On either sheet, ink is grayscale: the two hands already carry the state, so colour has nothing left to do there. The world is deliberately physical — procedurally creased paper, cast shadows, a rim-lit cut edge — but never illustrated.
keyCharacteristics: Two sheets of different grammage, lying at the same level on a table
Zero rules inside a sheet; whitespace and an aligned numeric column separate
Handwriting for intent, monospace for record, serif for pre-printed
Grayscale ink on both sheets; the table is the only warm thing in frame
Flat by default — depth is for objects that are genuinely separate
Amounts sit in one right-aligned tabular column in both states; only their truth-value changes

rules.name: The Grayscale Ink Rule
rules.body: Inside a sheet, ink is achromatic: tinta and verde both resolve to --ink-1 and become indistinguishable there, which is the point -- the typography already encodes who wrote each line, so colour would repeat a signal carried better. This governs content, not controls: the purchase tick keeps --verde-0 as an affordance, while names, quantities, brands, shops, prices and totals are ink. Outside a sheet the palette applies in full.

rules.name: The Surfaces-Keep-Their-Colour Rule
rules.body: Grayscale is a rule about content, not about the world. The table stays wooden and the paper stays cool-blue under it.

rules.name: The Dark Is A Re-Theme Rule
rules.body: Dark mode is authored, not inverted — a late-night desk: graphite paper, cream ink, accents lifted rather than flipped. Never derive one with filter: invert().

rules.name: The Cap-Height Rule
rules.body: Faces are matched by measured cap height, never by px value. Patrick Hand SC at 18.9px measures a 13.03px cap; JetBrains Mono only reaches that at 17.5px.

rules.name: The Tabular Numerals Rule
rules.body: Every figure in the amount column, plus every EAN, is tabular-nums in JetBrains Mono. Amounts must stay aligned across rows. Inline quantities on either sheet are exempt: they are not in the column, and on the unpurchased sheet they are handwriting, not a record.

rules.name: The Uppercase Tracking Rule
rules.body: All caps costs the word-shape cue people scan by, so tracking is always added back; never set caps at default tracking. How much depends on the face as much as the size (the hand is tracked wider than the mono despite setting larger), so each role carries its own figure in its typography entry and this rule names none of them. The single exception is hand meta text, which has no role of its own and takes 0.05em. Negative tracking belongs to large lowercase type only, at whatever value that role's entry carries, and never appears on caps; a negative value on an uppercase role is always a sign error.

rules.name: The Provisional Face Rule
rules.body: The hand face is not settled and an appearance section in settings is planned, so no face is hard-coded in a component.

rules.name: The Aisle Wins The Tie Rule
rules.body: When the composing-at-home moment and the in-store moment want different layouts, design for the store.

rules.name: The Ruled-Not-Raised Rule
rules.body: Separation inside a screen is a hairline or a tone change, never a shadow.

rules.name: The Two Sheets Exception
rules.body: The list and the receipt are genuinely separate physical objects, so each casts a real shadow — onto the table, not onto each other. The only sanctioned in-page shadow.

rules.name: The Cut-Edge Rule
rules.body: Anything representing paper gets the sheet radius (--r-sheet, rounded.sheet). Anything representing interface gets the interface scale. Never mix them on one element.

dos: Do set type through --font-* variables and size it from the scale; a runtime appearance setting is planned.
Do use the semantic type classes (.t-h2, .t-price, .t-caption) when adding new UI.
Do give every figure in the amount column, and every EAN, font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums in JetBrains Mono. Inline quantities are exempt.
Do match paired faces by measured cap height, not by px value.
Do keep touch targets at --hit-min (44px) or larger.
Do author dark-mode values by hand for every new colour.
Do add tracking whenever setting uppercase.
Do keep the accent rare — it marks what is interactive, not what is present.
donts: Don't write a raw font-size in px in a component.
Don't use the compatibility aliases in index.css (--text, --text-h, --bg2, --shadow).
Don't put a rule between rows inside a sheet.
Don't show a price on an unpurchased item unless it is a real recorded figure, styled as a hint.
Don't illustrate the paper metaphor — no torn edges, photographic texture, curled corners or stains.
Don't add a shadow between sections, rows or cards inside a screen.
Don't desaturate surfaces when applying the grayscale ink rule.
Don't derive dark mode with filter: invert().
Don't build a glossy consumer look: gradients, blobs, confetti, celebration on a checkmark.
Don't build an enterprise dashboard: dense sortable grids, sidebar nav, KPI tiles.
Don't style a store name as a brand — store names are user-entered data.
Don't ship feDisplacementMap text warping on the live list.
